findings.title: Ambiguity in Source-of-Truth Between run_artifacts and iee_artifacts
findings.rationale: Potential for drift and inconsistency between worker-internal artifacts and customer-facing artifacts, with unclear reconciliation/visibility for orphaned artifacts.
findings.evidence: Section 6.1.2 Schema: 'Promotion is not transactional across worker-internal iee_artifacts and customer-facing run_artifacts, so partial failure can leave a row in one and not the other. The MVP's posture: ... No automatic backfill of pre-MVP iee_artifacts rows; only artifacts produced after the MVP ships appear in run_artifacts.'

findings.title: PDF Rendering Determinism Contract Lacks Concrete Implementation/Validation Plan
findings.rationale: Contract claims byte-level determinism via normalization, but details for ensuring full determinism (e.g., handling potential @react-pdf/renderer output drift across environments) require more concrete specification and test automation.
findings.evidence: Section 4.4.3: 'The acceptance criterion in §9.1 ("byte-deterministic across re-runs") is satisfied via a small post-render normalization step in reportRenderingService' — but no details on automated golden testing or regression.

findings.title: Support Agent's Per-Ticket Atomic Claim TTL Has No Out-of-Band Recovery Worker
findings.rationale: Spec asserts TTL expiry is sufficient, but does not back this with monitoring or out-of-band clean up for pathological or lost-claim cases.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.4: 'On run abort, the claim ages out via the TTL — no recovery worker is required for Phase 1.'

findings.title: File Delivery Worker-to-S3 IAM Path Is Directionally Open
findings.rationale: There are two physically distinct upload paths for artifact bytes, and the spec leaves the selection to ops-time decision without fully accounting for migration costs or forward/backward compatibility across deployments.
findings.evidence: Section 6.1.4: 'Option A is the steady-state target; Option B is a viable Phase 1 fallback if worker-scoped IAM is not yet provisioned.'

findings.title: Prompt Override Controls Lean on Dev Discipline, Not Technical Enforcement
findings.rationale: Relies on server-side string scans and clear-text warnings for prompt-injection prevention, but does not have a formal list or enforcement mechanism robust to new injection vectors.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.6: 'The MVP applies four narrow controls...[but] these controls are dev-discipline, not a security boundary.'

findings.title: Classification Cache Table Not Fully Specified; Open Decision Gating Complete Implementation
findings.rationale: Final caching scheme for support.classify_ticket outputs is gated on unresolved Open Decision; migration and interface changes will be needed.
findings.evidence: Section 5.4.1 New skill: 'Classification results are cached by content hash of (ticket_id, last_message_external_id) in a new lightweight cache table or in agent_run_messages reuse. See Open Decision 11.4.'

findings.title: Eval Regression Set Requirements Not Fully Enforced in Acceptance Gates
findings.rationale: Regression evaluation falls open if fewer than two rows, or if Foundry export is unavailable/stale, with only operator warnings. This allows critical quality gates to be sidestepped without fail-closed behavior.
findings.evidence: Section 5.5.2: 'the MVP's posture is fail-open with explicit operator visibility, NOT fail-closed' and Section 7.3 'if fewer than two rows exist, the gate exits 0 (fail-open)'

findings.title: Concurrency of Agent Runs Relies on Advisory Locks and Filters But Has Residual Race Potential
findings.rationale: While the advisory lock and per-ticket claim minimize concurrency issues, brief race windows may exist, especially during high-throughput moments or distributed latency spikes.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.1: 'Two concurrent install attempts...the MVP defends against this with two layers...' and 'Concurrency under racing installs.'

findings.title: No End-to-End or Frontend Tests for Core User Workflows
findings.rationale: Test strategy is strictly pure/unit/integration, but omits end-to-end or UI regression checks for key user artifact delivery interactions, which may cause regressions to slip by undetected.
findings.evidence: Section 7.1: 'No frontend unit tests, no React component tests, no E2E tests of the Automation OS app. The framing assumption (`frontend_tests: none_for_now`...')

findings.title: Classification Scheme for confidence/intent/urgency Lacks Formal Schema Enforcement
findings.rationale: Skill outputs depend on model outputs and loose enums, without evidence of runtime contract enforcement (Zod parse or similar) for accidental null/undefined/malformed values.
findings.evidence: Section 5.4.1 New skill: 'output_schema: intent: enum ... confidence: number (0..1)' but no statement about runtime contract check.
